This has been of great aid to the north

All the lines are now hauling cmpties
west to relieve the lumbr situation. The
shipping season for lumber extends
throughout the year. but after the first
of next month orders will show a fall
ing off. Mill men are anxious to ret
their lumber to market and this is ere
ating heavy demand for cars.


1t was declared yesterday by a North
ern Pacific official that 20 per cent of
the west-bound trains were made up of
empties, which were being hurried 70
the, coast. to relieve tn shortag here.
Other lines have heavy proportion
of mpties in the west bound trains,
though the proportion will not run as
high.


The car shortage has been felt princi-
pally on the Seattle division of the
Northern Pacific. Complaints have
been coming in frequently from the mill
men of that secion, but heretofore it
